perturbation.original_text: Unless otherwise defined, capitalised terms, singular or plural, used in this Amendment, shall have the same meaning ascribed thereto in the GMA.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless otherwise defined herein, capitalized terms, singular or plural, used in this Amendment pertaining to invoicing, shall have the meaning defined in Clause 12, INVOICING AND PAYMENT TERMS, overriding any definition in the GMA.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that for invoicing-related matters, the definitions within Clause 12 of the Amendment take precedence over the GMA. This conflicts with the original blanket statement that all capitalized terms retain the meaning ascribed in the GMA, creating uncertainty. This directly introduces an inconsistency in interpreting terms related to payments. The contradicted location is Section 1, where the original definition of capitalized terms is located.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. DEFINITIONS Unless otherwise defined, capitalised terms, singular or plural, used in this Amendment, shall have the same meaning ascribed thereto in the GMA.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a contradiction. The original text states that capitalized terms in the Amendment have the same meaning as in the GMA. However, the changed text states that for invoicing matters, Clause 12 definitions override the GMA's definitions. This directly contradicts Section 1 (the contradicted text), which states that terms have the same meaning as ascribed in the GMA unless otherwise defined. The change introduces a specific exception that clashes with the general rule, thus creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Amendment shall enter into force on the date of its signature by both Parties and, unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by the Parties through a subsequent amendment to the GMA, shall remain in force for the term of the referenced GMA.
perturbation.changed_text: This Amendment shall enter into force on the date of its signature by both Parties and, unless terminated earlier by either Party with thirty (30) days written notice, shall remain in force for the term of the referenced GMA.
perturbation.explanation: This alteration introduces a contradiction regarding the duration of the Amendment. Initially, the Amendment's duration aligns with the GMA's term unless modified through a subsequent written agreement. The modified version allows either party to terminate the Amendment with thirty days' notice, creating a discrepancy with the original clause and uncertainty about the Amendment's longevity. The contradicted text is the original section stating that the agreement shall remain in force for the term of the referenced GMA. This also creates a conflict with Section 6.3 which states that the amendment shall not be modified except by a written document duly signed by both parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. EFFECTIVE DATE AND DURATION This Amendment shall enter into force on the date of its signature by both Parties and, unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by the Parties through a subsequent amendment to the GMA, shall remain in force for the term of the referenced GMA.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction because it allows for termination of the Amendment with 30 days written notice, while the original text and the contradicted text state that the Amendment remains in force for the term of the referenced GMA unless modified by a subsequent written agreement. This creates a direct conflict regarding the duration and termination conditions of the Amendment, violating the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition because the same clause is being repeated in multiple locations with conflicting implications. The initial duration aligned with the GMA's term, now either party can terminate with notice, creating uncertainty about the longevity of the amendment. This uncertainty and conflicting clause meet the criteria for a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Amendment contains the entire agreement between the Parties regarding the subject-matter and shall supersede any previous understandings, commitments and/or representations whatsoever oral or written.
perturbation.changed_text: This Amendment contains the entire agreement between the Parties regarding the subject-matter, except for any pre-existing oral agreements made prior to January 1, 2015, which shall remain in full force and effect, and shall supersede any previous understandings, commitments and/or representations whatsoever oral or written.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that pre-existing oral agreements prior to a specific date will still be valid and supersede previous written agreements, the change contradicts the initial statement that this amendment contains the 'entire agreement' and supersedes 'any previous understandings...whatsoever oral or written'. This creates uncertainty as it's unclear which agreements are truly binding. The contradicted text is the original section stating that the amendment contains the entire agreement and supersedes previous agreements. This creates an ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1 This Amendment contains the entire agreement between the Parties regarding the subject-matter and shall supersede any previous understandings, commitments and/or representations whatsoever oral or written.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly carves out an exception for pre-existing oral agreements from the original clause stating that the amendment contains the 'entire agreement' and supersedes 'any previous understandings...whatsoever oral or written.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, creating a clear and irreconcilable inconsistency. This invalidates the 'entire agreement' clause. The original text said that 'This Amendment contains the entire agreement between the Parties regarding the subject-matter and shall supersede any previous understandings, commitments and/or representations whatsoever oral or written.' and the contradicted text is '6.1 This Amendment contains the entire agreement between the Parties regarding the subject-matter and shall supersede any previous understandings, commitments and/or representations whatsoever oral or written.'
